calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kathryn Murphy appeared via zoom for Barabara Dahl (executor).

Ms. Murphy stated that she has suffered from 2 falls, that she ended up in the hospital, and that she is trying to find a good fit to take on this case. Both matters are ordered off calendar. Status on close of estate is set for September 15, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Lozada. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Continued To Sep-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Kathryn Murphy (attorney) to appear on September 23, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear. Status on Close of Estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Continued To Aug-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathryn Murphy for and with Barbara Dahl, Executor (via Zoom).

Ms. Murphy reported that she has been working to resolve issues related to an IRA account funded through an LLC investment, which complicates accounting and tax obligations under applicable regulations. She stated that the investment is currently being carried at zero dollars by the management company and that she anticipates resolution of the matter with the custodian in Texas within a few weeks.

The Court inquired whether obtaining a waiver of accounting might be more efficient, given that the remaining assets are expected to be distributed to the university after statutory fees and costs. Ms. Murphy agreed to explore that option and noted that the university, which holds the beneficial interest through a charitable foundation, may be willing to waive.

The Court further inquired about prior indications that counsel intended to substitute out of the case. Ms. Murphy confirmed that remains the plan and that she is currently consulting with a potential replacement while keeping the university informed.

The Court continued the matter to August 26, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Continued To Jun-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (personal):
Kathryn Murphy, Esq., for and with Barbara Dahl, Executor.

At the outset, the court acknowledges counsel's response to the court's Order to Show Cause. Ms. Murphy reports that she does intend to substitute out as attorney in this matter and that she has a few candidates to take this matter from her citing that she believes it should be counsel familiar with tax issues. Counsel requests a continuance of thirty (30) days to allow the preceding to occur.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jun-17-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The remaining matters are taken off calendar. The court confirms with Ms. Murphy that if she does decide to substitute out as counsel, it would be preferable that she did so prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Why Executor Barbara Dahl Should Not Be Suspended As Executor For Failing To Appear
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Continued To May-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Kathryn Murphy (attorney) to appear on May 6, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear. An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Barbara Dahl (Executor) to appear on May 6, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why she should not be suspended for failure to appear. Status on Close of Estate and Order of Status Hearing are also continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Continued To May-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Continued To Apr-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathryn M. Murphy for Barbara Dahl, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court noted the unusually high number of appearances for this case, this being the 18th calling.
Ms. Murphy explained that recent disruptions in her office, including a death in the family and staffing challenges, contributed to the continued delays. She stated that a small but material discrepancy in the estate accounting remains unresolved. Ms. Murphy confirmed that a new paralegal with extensive experience has been hired to assist with the accounting and expressed optimism that the matter can be resolved within 60 days. The court instructed Ms. Murphy not to delay filing the petition over minor discrepancies, as the court has the discretion to waive such issues if the overall accounting is sound.

The court granted a 60-day continuance, scheduling the next hearing for April 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court shall issue an order for the executor, Barbara Dahl, to appear on April 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 unless the final petition is filed before that date, in which case the order will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Continued To Feb-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathryn M. Murphy for Barbara Dahl, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court noted that this is the 17th time the status of closing the estate has been called. Ms. Murphy explained that she intends to file and serve a petition within one week. The court expressed concern over prior delays and stated that similar assurances had been made in the past.

The court inquired whether Mr. Murphy would remain as counsel in this matter. Ms. Murphy indicated that she intends to complete the work necessary to move the case forward.

The court granted Ms. Murphy's request for additional time and continued the matter to February 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court ordered Ms. Murphy to file a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration prior to the next calling of the case and warned that the court will consider issuing sanctions should she fail to comply.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Feb-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathryn M. Murphy for Barbara Dahl, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Ms. Murphy reported submitting a declaration before midnight the previous night, which the court indicated it had not received. Ms. Murphy disclosed that her wife suffered a heart attack on January 11, 2025, requiring hospitalization and surgeries. She requested two additional weeks to consult with her client regarding the case's status and determine if another attorney might need to take over representation.

The court acknowledged Ms. Murphy's circumstances but noted that this was the 16th appearance without a petition having been filed, and that the case was last on calendar on December 10 with insufficient progress made since then.

The court ordered that $250 in sanctions shall be imposed on Ms. Murphy under CCP 177.5 due to repeated delays. The court continued the status hearing to February 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed Ms. Murphy to file a petition prior to that date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-05-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Kathy Murphy appearing via zoom for (Executor) Barbara Dahl.

The Court ask for status on final inventory and appraisal. Mrs. Murphy stated that this is a probate of pour over will with an IRA with no beneficiary designated and is mainly a share of an LLC which has limited transferability and the difficulty with the terms under the membership agreement. Mrs. Murphy also stated that it was purchased in 2019 in $78,000, that in 2021 the capital was reduced to $16,000, and that she recently learned that the underline LLC maybe defunct. Further discussion was held regarding their being no funds to hire counsel in the estate for this issue whether or not it appropriate to consider abandonment, the other assets being significant e-bonds that were cashed out taxes and expenses to be paid the executor not having access to the financial aspect of the company the need to confirm, that the university foundation would not object to some form of abandonment whether the LLC IRA ought to be written off as it is not a positive asset etc., The Court asked counsel to file a partial inventory and appraisal putting in a range from $0 to $78,000. Mrs. Murphy stated that there will be an accounting, that notice has been given pursuant to Probate code 9202, that there is no issue of the decedent paying bills as he paid his own medical bills until the end of his life, and that they don't have a list of creditors to be concerned about. The executors report and administration is off calendar. The Status on close of estate is set for June 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of the inventory and appraisal is also set for the same date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: H. Singh; not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: No appearance required. Pro forma hearing only for purposes of admitting will.

This matter is on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will, the Court noting that notice of the proceedings appears to be complete and that no objection has been made to the Petition. The Court grants the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The Will dated April 19, 2011 is admitted to probate. Barbara Dahl is appointed the Executor of the estate with full authority under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Bond is waived. Order for the same is signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for hearing on June 22, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition to Request Additional Time to Administer Estate is filed 1 week before that date, the matter will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
